rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Invalidate The Williams Family Trust Of Betty Yvonne Williams And Leroy Williams And To Remove The Appointed Trustee, Shawny K. Willimas Under Unde Influence And Perjury And Appoint Pltf As New Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues remain: 1) At least 30 days before the time set for hearing on the petition, the petitioner shall cause notice of the hearing to be delivered pursuant to Section 1215 to all trustees and all beneficiaries (Probate Code Section 17203). 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 4) No proposed order has been received (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF GERALD DERYAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] The Declaration of Matthew DeRyan in support of the motion was filed December 29, 2025. The declaration states Mr. DeRyan and Ms. Steele had a meeting December 10, 2024 in which Ms. Steele stated "all documents for the estate were destroyed" by attorney Marc Seidenfeld. The declaration also states Mr. DeRyan served "Requests for Production of Documents, on the party designation and name of responding party" on December 20, 2025 "plus previous dates." He states he made "subsequent requests for production of said requested material" were made to Trustee and "Attorney of the Estate (Anne Steele) on March 11, 2025, March 18, 2025, August 21, 2025, August 22, 2025, August 26, 2025, October 9, 2025, November 4, 2025, December 20, 2025, and December 29, 2025. The declaration states he has not received any response to his Requests for Production of Documents "from the responding parties." The underlying petition was filed October 9, 2025. The papers state requests for production of documents were made on dates both before and after the filing date. It is not clear what form the requests for documents took after the filing date, or whether the requests were presented in writing, or served, or on whom. It is also not clear from the papers whether Ms. Steele and Mr. Russell were served identical requests for production of documents, or what documents were sought from either of them. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF GERALD DERYAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] It does not appear from the papers that the request for production of documents was formed and presented in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.030, as discussed below. The Civil Discovery Act provides the requirements for a request for production of documents, in Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.030: (a)(1) A party demanding inspection, copying, testing, or sampling shall number each set of demands consecutively. (2) A party demanding inspection, copying, testing, or sampling of electronically stored information may specify the form or forms in which each type of electronically stored information is to be produced. (b) In the first paragraph immediately below the title of the case, there shall appear the identity of the demanding party, the set number, and the identity of the responding party. (c) Each demand in a set shall be separately set forth, identified by number or letter, and shall do all of the following: (1) Designate the documents, tangible things, land or other property, or electronically stored information to be inspected, copied, tested, or sampled either by specifically describing each individual item or by reasonably particularizing each category of item. (2) Specify a reasonable time for the inspection, copying, testing, or sampling that is at least 30 days after service of the demand, unless the court for good cause shown has granted leave to specify an earlier date. In an unlawful detainer action or other proceeding under Chapter 4 (commencing with Section 1159) of Title 3 of Part 3, the demand shall specify a reasonable time for the inspection, copying, testing, or sampling that is at least five days after service of the demand, unless the court, for good cause shown, has granted leave to specify an earlier date. (3) Specify a reasonable place for making the inspection, copying, testing, or sampling, and performing any related activity. (4) Specify any inspection, copying, testing, sampling, or related activity that is being demanded, as well as the manner in which that activity will be performed, and whether that activity will permanently alter or destroy the item involved. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.040, "The party making a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling shall serve a copy of the demand on the party to whom it is directed and on all other parties who have appeared in the action." In the document titled "Motion to Compel: (Request for production of documents, and validation of one Exhibit as evidence (to be validated by Fiduciary Aishlyn O'Coyle & Former Attorney Marc Seidenfeld - authors of said email)," Movant states this Motion seeks the following: (1) "All medical records of Gerald's between 2015-2018 including the time at the nursing home located in Daly City, Ca.[.]" [emphasis in original]; (2) "Any evidence, including video - that would corroborate the (as alleged by Petitioner DeRyan) false allegations via email that he (Petitioner) - even came to his Uncle Gerald's house from him (Petitioner's) home address (in Washington State) to steal from Gerald[.]" and (3) "An accurate, true, correct and updated list to be provided to Petitioner Matthew DeRyan and for the court of All persons entitled to notices." The Memorandum specifically names Anne Steele, attorney for the estate of Gerald DeRyan, as a "respondent" and alleges Ms. Steele failed to respond to Movant's discovery request for production of certain documents, specifically an updated list of trust beneficiaries. Trustee Tom Rusell is also mentioned, with Ms. Steele, as having allegedly engaged in "a deliberate act of sabotage, negligence & fraud" by not responding to the discovery request.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF GERALD DERYAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Request S For Production; Set Numbers; Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Michael B. McNaughton. Before the court is Petitioner Matthew DeRyan's Motion to Compel: (Request for production of documents, and validation of one Exhibit as evidence (to be validated by Fiduciary Aishlyn O'Coyle & Former Attorney Marc Seidenfeld - authors of said email), filed December 29, 2025 ("Motion"). The Motion is DENIED without prejudice, as premature. The Discovery Act requires that "at least one defendant has been served with the summons and complaint" before any discovery proceedings may commence (California Shellfish Inc. v. United Shellfish Co. (1997) 56 Cal. App. 4th 16, 22). No discovery is conducted unless some adverse party has notice of the litigation, and of the particular discovery proceeding, and an opportunity to protect adversarial interests. (California Shellfish Inc. v. United Shellfish Co. (1997) 56 Cal. App. 4th 16, 23-24). There is no showing on the record that the underlying petition, filed October 9, 2025 and titled "Refiled, Revised and Correct Petition to Contest Living Trust of Gerald DeRyan Probate Code 17200" and its supporting memorandum and declarations, has been properly served. It appears from the document titled "Proof of Service and Proof of Copy of Summons on Respondents," also filed October 9, 2025, that at most the persons listed on the attached Notice of Hearing - Decedent's Estate or Trust received an unfiled copy of the Petition to Contest Living Trust - Refiled, Amended, Corrected by mail on October 3, 2025. No hearing date is listed on the Notice of Hearing because the October 3, 2025 mailing date is before the October 9, 2025 filing date; a hearing date for the petition would be generated only after the petition was filed. The Summons forms included with the Proof of Service also do not list a hearing date or case number and were not issued by the court. This is not sufficient service of the pleadings on the respondent parties to activate the right to conduct discovery in this matter. As to the Motion itself, there are also substantive defects that prevent the court from issuing an order to compel production of documents. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.020(b), "A plaintiff may make a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling without leave of court at any time that is 10 days after the service of the summons on, or appearance by, the party to whom the demand is directed, whichever occurs first." As discussed above, it does not appear that the underlying petition has been properly served on anyone, and specifically not on the people named in the Motion, Anne Steele and Thomas Russell. Neither Anne Steele nor Thomas Russell have made a general appearance in this matter, although an attorney for Thomas Russell has attended hearings in this matter on special appearance. Per the Notice of Hearing attached to the document titled "Notice of Motion for Continuances as Approved By Kenny," filed February 6, 2026, it appears notice of the Motion, and the April1, 2026 hearing at 2:00 p.m., was served on people entitled to notice of this matter. There is no separate notice of motion. The document titled Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support for Motion/Order Compelling Responses to Request for Production ("Memorandum"), cites to Code of Civil Procedure sections 2031.010, 2023.030, and 2031.300 as authority to bring the Motion, and Sections 2018.010 et seq and 2031.300(a) regarding wavier of objections by failing to serve timely response. Monetary sanctions are sought pursuant to Section 2023.030. This is facially a motion to compel production of documents.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOURDES MUNOZ AGUIRRE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 10/12/27. The successor administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 10/12/27. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed. NB: Nothing has been filed yet to confirm who the post-deceased heir's successor-in-interest is. Letters issued to the personal representative of Leoncio Munoz's estate (or an original 13100 affidavit, as the case may be) will need to be filed before final distribution will be ordered.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RUBLY FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 20, 1988, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee To Fill Vacancy
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
